compiled_state.description:
## Finding (DE3 — verified)
`_dispatch_mutation` is dead in production (self-documented as "no longer called from reconcile_once") but kept alive only for a coverage test, and it pins a lazily-built dispatch table — giving false coverage confidence and drift risk vs the real applier path.

Citations: `src/rebar/_engine/rebar_reconciler/reconcile.py:83` (`_dispatch_mutation`), self-doc at `reconcile.py:944-946`; dispatch table at `reconcile.py:25`; only caller `tests/unit/rebar_reconciler/orchestrate/test_dispatch_coverage.py`.

## Remediation
Delete `_dispatch_mutation` and its coverage-only dispatch table; rewrite/delete `test_dispatch_coverage.py` so coverage reflects the live dispatch path only. If the table is needed at a live site, inline it there.

cascade_flag: symbol deletion — confirm no non-test caller remains.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `_dispatch_mutation` and its dead dispatch table are removed
- [ ] `test_dispatch_coverage.py` exercises the real dispatch path (or is removed)
- [ ] Coverage no longer counts the fossil path
compiled_state.title: Delete dead _dispatch_mutation + coverage-only dispatch table
